celestialBodies.name: Sol
celestialBodies.description: The Sun, also known as Sol, is the primary of the crystal sphere known as Realmspace. It is a size H spherical fire body, and is orbited by 8 planets, their satellites and a variety of asteroids, comets and nebulae. Within the Sun's immediate orbit there are twelve sargassos.

celestialBodies.name: Anadia
celestialBodies.description: Anadia, referred to as one of the Dawn Heralds by the natives of Toril, is a size B spherical earth body, and is known for its xenophobic natives.

celestialBodies.name: Coliar
celestialBodies.description: Coliar, referred to as one of the Dawn Heralds by the natives of Toril, is a size G spherical air body, and is inhabited by lizardfolk, aarakocra and dragons.

celestialBodies.name: Toril
celestialBodies.description: Toril is a size E spherical earth body, and is one of the most populous worlds in the system. It is orbited by a single moon, named Selûne, and an asteroid cluster, known as the Tears of Selûne.

celestialBodies.name: Selune
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Karpri
celestialBodies.description: Karpri, referred to as one of the Five Wanderers by the natives of Toril, is a size D spherical water body orbited by an artificial elven base.[

celestialBodies.name: Chandos
celestialBodies.description: Chandos, referred to as one of the Five Wanderers by the natives of Toril, is a size F spherical water body with numerous unstable land formations that are prone to collapse or change at a moments notice.

celestialBodies.name: Glyth
celestialBodies.description: Glyth, referred to as one of the Five Wanderers by the natives of Toril, is a size E spherical earth body and is dominated by mind flayers. It has a large planetary ring system and three moons.

celestialBodies.name: Haven
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Mingabwe
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Polluter
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Garden
celestialBodies.description: Garden, referred to as one of the Five Wanderers by the natives of Toril, is a cluster of size A asteroids which share a common atmosphere and are bound together by a colossal plant known as Yggdrasil's Child. Garden is classified as a liveworld by the Shou, and has twelve moons.

celestialBodies.name: Grandchild
celestialBodies.description: Grandchild is a size B elliptical earth body. Its off-white surface is perfectly smooth, without any meteorite pock marks, and reflects the light eerily.

celestialBodies.name: Yerthad
celestialBodies.description: Yerthad is a size C spherical earth body. Its atmospheric envelope extends about 100 miles above its surface, and is extremely toxic as well as caustic. The atmosphere consists primarily of sulfuric acid, which covers the moon's surface in a sea of acid.

celestialBodies.name: Peaceon
celestialBodies.description: Peaceon is a size B planar-shaped earth body. The moon once served as a stopping point for spacefaring elves, but has since long been deserted.

celestialBodies.name: Retinae
celestialBodies.description: Retinae is a size C spherical earth body. The moon resembles an eye ball: where the pupil would be is a large crater where a rare type of fungus can be found that is useful in the preparation of spells and potions related to the improvement of vision.

celestialBodies.name: Glorianus
celestialBodies.description: Glorianus is a size A crescent-shaped earth body, completely covered with a soft green grass. The human druid who named the moon, Griffon Broadleaf, makes his home there to this day.

celestialBodies.name: Fjord
celestialBodies.description: Fjord is a size B spherical earth body. The moon is a voidworld, without an atmosphere, and was once a great mining colony.

celestialBodies.name: Locci
celestialBodies.description: Locci is a size A rectangular earth body, and is a voidworld, without an atmosphere. The rectangular moon's edges and corners have been chipped by meteorites.

celestialBodies.name: Dragon Rock
celestialBodies.description: Dragon Rock is a size C earth body. Basically a roughly shaped statue of a white dragon, Dragon Rock was created by a mage named Darkon of the Earth’s Depths, while he was delving into the secrets of the planet below. The moon is made entirely of white jade, and has a thin atmosphere that smells faintly of pipe smoke.

celestialBodies.name: Knurl
celestialBodies.description: Knurl is a size B spherical earth body. The moon is made entirely of marble swirling with various colors. There are chunks of the surface missing, where liquid marble oozes in a slow stream. This oozing marble can be moulded into sculptures, which harden within days of being removed from the moon.

celestialBodies.name: Sunson
celestialBodies.description: Sunson is a size C spherical fire body which produces enough heat to warm wildspace within 1,000 miles (1,609 kilometres). The fire moon produces enough light to illuminate Garden during the night, and burns almost twice as hot as the Sun.

celestialBodies.name: Templar
celestialBodies.description: Templar is a size B disc-shaped earth body. The moon serves as an outpost for a hundred mages of various disciplines who are studying Garden, hoping to uncover its secrets. They finance their operation by producing and selling magic items.

celestialBodies.name: Farworld
celestialBodies.description: Farworld is a size C spherical earth body which serves as a trading post. Despite being far away from most inhabited worlds, it does a surprising amount of trade. Almost anything non-magical can be found here for sale.

celestialBodies.name: H'Catha
celestialBodies.description: H'Catha, referred to as one of the Five Wanderers by the natives of Toril, is a size C disk-shaped water world with an enormous mountain jutting out of its central hub. The planet is dominated by a number of beholder nations, and has two moons.

celestialBodies.name: Turnbetl
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Lumbe
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: Caer Windlauer
celestialBodies.description: A newly added planetary body.

celestialBodies.name: K'Thoutek
celestialBodies.description: K'Thoutek is a large cometary body about 300 miles (483 kilometers) in diameter, and is primarily made up of iron and frozen water. It travels around the Sun in an extremely elliptical orbit that is almost perpendicular to the ecliptic, coming as close as 15 million miles (24 million kilometers) to the sun, and travelling as far away as 200 million miles (322 million kilometers). It takes K'Thoutek just over 237 years to complete one revolution around the sun.[

celestialBodies.name: King Killer Star
celestialBodies.description: The King-Killer Star is a reddish cometary body that orbits the Sun and periodically appears in the skies of Toril. In localized regions, the King-Killer Star is visible every few decades, while every few hundred years it is visible across the continent of Faerûn. The King-Killer Star is linked to the Dracorage mythal, and its appearances in the skies of Toril causes dragons to go mad with rage, destroying and killing without reason.

celestialBodies.name: Skull of the Void
celestialBodies.description: The Skull of the Void is located about 50 million miles (80 million kilometers) from the crystal shell, and remains in a fixed position relative to the sun. The object is 12 miles (19 km) high and 10 miles (16 km) wide and resembles a human skull exactly. The enormous structure appears to be made of bone, and is able to repair itself if damaged in any way. It also constantly generates its own air supply.
